metadata.title: What the Floor Hides
metadata.setting: A small, quiet town where a young man takes a weekend job clearing out a long-abandoned house and uncovers a fifty-year-old secret.

characters.name: Luca Marr
characters.description: A 19-year-old with a lean build and dark hair who is restless and always needs to know the answer to things.
characters.flaw: Too curious — he cannot leave an unexplained thing alone, even when he should.
characters.current_state: suspicious and alert; has lied to Ray, returned home with the locked box, and concluded Ray's call was not a coincidence
key_items: locked metal box (COLE scratched on side)

characters.name: Ray Brennan
characters.description: A cautious, businesslike man in his fifties who owns the old house and hired Luca to clear it out.
characters.flaw: Secretive — he gives information only when he must.
characters.current_state: suspiciously calm; called Luca the same morning the box was found and asked about 'value', then ended the call quickly after Luca said no

locations.name: The Cole House
locations.description: A two-storey abandoned house on the edge of Harden, with dusty rooms, broken windows, and dark wooden floorboards.

locations.name: Harden Town
locations.description: A small, quiet town with a public library, a few old shops, and houses that have stood for over a hundred years.

locations.name: Luca's Flat
locations.description: A small rented room above a hardware shop in Harden, where Luca lives alone.

episode_history.summary: Ray Brennan meets Luca at the Cole House, hands him a key, and tells him firmly to bag and throw away everything inside, refusing to name the family who lived there or answer any questions. While clearing the back room, Luca finds a small video camera hidden under a pile of dusty curtains; he takes it home, charges it via a cable, and plays the tape. The tape shows a man and a frightened woman hiding a metal box under a loose floorboard in a room Luca recognises as the Cole House, and the episode ends with Luca picking up the house key.

Luca returns to the Cole House and methodically searches each room until he finds the one matching the tape: pale walls, window on the left, and dark wooden floorboards. He lifts a loose board near the far wall and pulls out a small, locked metal box with the name 'COLE' scratched into its side. Ray Brennan calls that same morning and asks, too casually, whether Luca found anything of value; Luca lies and says no, then takes the box back to his flat, where he concludes that Ray's timing was not a coincidence.

unresolved_threads.description: What is inside the metal box hidden under the loose floorboard in the Cole House?
Why was Ray Brennan so insistent that Luca keep nothing from the house?
Who are the man and woman shown on the tape hiding the metal box — and does the name COLE scratched on the box identify them?
Why did Ray Brennan call asking about 'value' on the exact morning Luca found the metal box, and how much does Ray already know?

series_plan.central_question: What secret did the family hide in the metal box fifty years ago, and who in Harden is protecting that secret today?

arc_beats.beat: Luca finds an old video camera in the abandoned house; the tape inside shows the previous family hiding a locked metal box under the floorboards of the very room he is standing in.

Luca lifts the floorboard and finds the real metal box, but it is locked; he also sees a name scratched on the box — Cole; his employer Ray Brennan calls and asks, too casually, whether Luca found anything of value in the house.
At the town library Luca learns the Cole family left Harden suddenly in 1974 and never came back; when he returns to his flat that evening, the metal box has been stolen.
Luca finds evidence pointing to an elderly woman, Mrs. Ward, whose late husband was a close friend of Ray Brennan and once did business with the Cole family; Luca goes to her house.

Mrs. Ward admits the truth: fifty years ago her husband took money that belonged to the Cole family, forcing them to leave town; the box holds a signed document and the cash that prove it; Brennan knew and stayed silent.
Luca locates the Cole family's descendants and returns the box to them; Mrs. Ward and Brennan face the consequences of the secret; the central question is answered and all threads are closed.

last_scene_position: Luca is sitting at his table in his flat (loc_003) with the locked metal box in front of him, having just returned from the Cole House. He has found the box under the loose floorboard in the back room, read the name 'COLE' scratched into its side, lied to Ray Brennan on the phone, replaced the loose board, and locked the house behind him. It remains unresolved what is inside the locked box, who the Cole family are, and how Ray knew to call the exact morning Luca made the discovery.
